content.problemStatement:
Given an N×N chessboard and a knight at position (Sx,Sy),(Sx, Sy),(Sx,Sy), calculate the probability that the knight will remain on the board after making exactly K moves.

In each move, the knight follows standard chess rules, moving in an L-shape pattern. From any position (x, y), the knight can move to any of these 8 positions:

(x+2, y+1), (x+2, y-1)
(x-2, y+1), (x-2, y-1)
(x+1, y+2), (x+1, y-2)
(x-1, y+2), (x-1, y-2)

Each of the 8 possible moves is equally likely.


Input Format

First line contains TTT - the number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains two integers NNN and KKK - board size and number of moves
Second line contains two integers SxSxSx and SySySy - initial position




Output Format

For each test case, print the probability with exactly 6 decimal places

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:

From (0,0), only 2 out of 8 possible moves stay on a 3×3 board
Probability = 2/8 = 0.25

Test Case 2:

After two moves, only 4 out of 64 possible paths stay on board
Probability = 4/64 = 0.0625
